title: Corns and Calluses
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Corns and calluses are caused by repeated pressure or friction.
They often appear on feet, toes, hands, or other rubbed areas.
Reducing pressure or friction and protecting the skin can help.
Foot wounds, infection, diabetes, poor circulation, or neuropathy require a different pathway.
